title: quercus agrifolia var. oxyadenia
intro: Quercus agrifolia var. oxyadenia, also known as the Santa Cruz oak, is a subspecies of the California live oak (Quercus agrifolia), a deciduous or evergreen tree native to the Pacific coast of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus agrifolia var. oxyadenia include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, can be made from the leaves, bark, and twigs, and are often used to promote wound healing, soothe digestive issues, and calm respiratory problems.
